title: matoniales
intro: The rank Matoniales is an order of plants, specifically a monotypic order in the flowering plant division, consisting of a single family, Matoniaceae. This order is characterized by its unique combination of characteristics, including the presence of two cotyledons and a single leaf at the top of a stem. The Matoniales order includes the family Matoniaceae, which contains the genus Matonia, as well as a few other families such as the family Orchidaceae, which includes the orchids, the family Euphorbiaceae, which includes the spurge family, and the family Cyperaceae, which includes the sedges. The Matoniales order is found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world.

families.family_desc: Matoniaceae is a family of extinct seed ferns, characterized by their broad leaves and club-shaped cones.
